title: PuzzlePod Agent Profile
description: Schema for validating PuzzlePod agent profile YAML files. Profiles define per-agent access control including filesystem permissions, executable allowlists, resource limits, network policy, behavioral triggers, and failure modes.

name.description: Unique identifier for the profile. Must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens.

description.description: Human-readable description of the profile's purpose and scope.

filesystem.description: Filesystem access control lists applied via Landlock LSM.

read_allowlist.description: List of absolute paths the agent is permitted to read. Paths are recursive (include all descendants). Glob segments (e.g. *) may appear in path strings.

write_allowlist.description: List of absolute paths the agent is permitted to write. Paths are recursive. The agent's branch working directory is always implicitly writable.

denylist.description: List of absolute paths the agent is denied access to. Denylist takes precedence over allowlists.

read_denylist.description: Paths explicitly denied for reading. Takes precedence over read_allowlist (defense in depth).

write_denylist.description: Paths explicitly denied for writing. Takes precedence over write_allowlist.

exec_allowlist.description: List of absolute paths to executables the agent is permitted to run. Glob patterns (e.g., /usr/bin/*) are supported.

exec_denylist.description: Executables denied from running (basename or path). Denylist takes precedence over exec_allowlist.

capabilities.description: List of Linux capabilities (e.g., CAP_NET_RAW) to grant to the agent process. Empty list means no additional capabilities beyond the minimal set.

resource_limits.description: Resource limits enforced via cgroups v2 and XFS project quotas.

memory_bytes.description: Maximum memory in bytes for the agent's cgroup. Minimum 16 MiB, maximum 64 GiB.

cpu_shares.description: CPU shares (relative weight) for the agent's cgroup. Higher values get proportionally more CPU time.

io_weight.description: I/O weight (relative priority) for the agent's cgroup. Maps to cgroup v2 io.weight.

max_pids.description: Maximum number of processes (PIDs) the agent may create. Enforced via cgroup v2 pids.max.

storage_quota_mb.description: Maximum storage in megabytes for the agent's OverlayFS upper layer. Enforced via XFS project quotas.

inode_quota.description: Maximum number of inodes (files/directories) for the agent's OverlayFS upper layer. Enforced via XFS project quotas.

network.description: Network access policy enforced via network namespaces and nftables.

mode.description: Network access mode. Blocked: no network access. Gated: only allowed_domains permitted via HTTP proxy. Monitored: all traffic allowed but logged. Unrestricted: no restrictions.

allowed_domains.description: List of domain names (or wildcard patterns like *.example.com) permitted when mode is Gated. Use ["*"] for gated proxy without domain filtering. Ignored for other modes.

behavioral.description: Behavioral monitoring thresholds enforced via fanotify. Triggers alert or kill when thresholds are exceeded.

max_deletions.description: Maximum number of file deletions before triggering a behavioral alert. Prevents mass-deletion attacks.

max_reads_per_minute.description: Maximum file read operations per minute before triggering a behavioral alert. Prevents data exfiltration via rapid enumeration.

credential_access_alert.description: Whether to raise an alert when the agent attempts to access credential files (SSH keys, tokens, .env files).

fail_mode.description: Behavior when governance evaluation fails or puzzled encounters an error. FailClosed: rollback pending changes (default). FailSilent: hold last known safe state. FailOperational: continue with reduced capability. FailSafeState: enter controlled safe state (for safety-critical deployments).

seccomp_mode.description: seccomp strategy: Permissive (default-allow + denylist) or Strict (default-deny + syscall allowlist).

allow_symlinks.description: Whether symlink creation is allowed in agent changesets. When false, symlink writes are rejected.
